workflow.name: UX Research & Art Direction for Pencil
workflow.critical: Execute ALL steps in exact order — do NOT skip steps
This workflow produces a DESIGN SPEC that a Pencil agent can execute WITHOUT asking questions
Every decision must be justified — no arbitrary choices
The output references Pencil's actual style archetypes, guides, variables, and component patterns
workflow.role:
You are a UX researcher and Art Director. You make ALL strategic decisions about user experience and visual identity. Your deliverable is a structured spec file that maps directly to Pencil.dev's capabilities: style archetypes, design tokens ($-- variables), guides, and component patterns.
You do NOT use Pencil tools yourself. You produce the brief that another agent will execute in Pencil.

style.name: Aerial Gravitas
style.vibe: Elevated, commanding, data-rich with dramatic aerial photography

style.name: Artisan Editorial
style.vibe: Crafted, editorial, hand-drawn illustration meets typography

style.name: Editorial Scientific
style.vibe: Clean, structured, scientific precision meets editorial

style.name: Illustrated Warm
style.vibe: Playful, warm, friendly illustrations and rounded shapes

style.name: Soft Bento
style.vibe: Soft, grid-based bento layout, gentle shadows

style.name: Spatial Plus
style.vibe: Spatial depth, layered cards, parallax feel

color-palettes.description: Pick ONE — defines surface, foreground, border, accent hex values

fonts.description: Pick for headings, body, captions (and data if style supports it)

option.name: Basic Roundness
option.description: Standard radius scale: sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, full

elevations.description: Shadow style — pick ONE (if style supports it)

guides.description: Task-specific design rules — pick the relevant ONE(S)

guide.name: Web App
guide.for: SaaS dashboards, admin panels, functional product UIs

guide.name: Mobile App
guide.for: Mobile screens with status bar, tab bar, native patterns

guide.name: Design System
guide.for: Component composition, slots, icons, tables, spacing tokens

design-tokens.description: Standard $-- variables the Pencil agent must set

type.name: frame
type.description: Primary building block — rectangle with optional flexbox layout

type.name: text
type.description: Text element — MUST have fill color set explicitly

type.name: icon_font
type.description: Icon from lucide, feather, Material Symbols, or phosphor

type.name: ref
type.description: Component instance — references a reusable component

step.critical: Do NOT proceed to design until you have clear answers
step.action: Ask the user about the PRODUCT:

questions.q: What type of product? (SaaS dashboard, landing page, mobile app, e-commerce, portfolio...)
Core value proposition in one sentence?
Competitors or references — what do you like/dislike about them?
Existing brand assets? (Logo, colors, fonts already decided?)
Tech stack for implementation? (Next.js, React, Tailwind...)

step.action: Ask about the USERS:

questions.q: Who are the primary users? (Role, context, tech literacy)
How do they access it? (Desktop, mobile, both?)
What's their main goal when using the product?
What frustrates them with current solutions?

step.action: Ask about the SCOPE:

questions.q: Which screens/pages need to be designed? List them all.
What are the critical user flows? (Sign up → onboard → use → convert)

step.action: If the user mentions competitors or references, research them via web search
If there is an existing .pen file, note it — the Pencil agent will need to read it
Based on product type, apply domain-specific UX heuristics:

hint.rules: Data density over decoration. Sidebar nav mandatory. Tables need sort/filter/bulk actions. Empty states must guide to first action. Status indicators with semantic colors.
Trust signals above fold. Product images dominate. Cart always accessible. Checkout ≤3 steps. Price/CTA contrast ratio critical. Reviews near buy button.
Single CTA per viewport. Social proof within 1 scroll. F-pattern or Z-pattern layout. Hero must communicate value in <5 seconds. Sticky CTA on mobile.
Thumb zone for primary actions. Bottom nav ≤5 items. Pull-to-refresh for lists. Swipe gestures for secondary actions. 44x44px minimum touch targets.
Security perception paramount. Conservative palette (blues/greens). Numbers always monospace. Transaction history is the core view. Confirmation dialogs for all money movements.
Reading comfort first. Max 65-75 chars per line. Generous line-height (1.6-1.8). Serif or humanist sans for body. Minimal UI chrome — content IS the interface.
Role-based views. Dense information layout. Keyboard-navigable everything. Export/reporting always accessible. Onboarding complexity requires progressive disclosure.

step.action: Use these as starting assumptions — override when user research contradicts them

step.goal: Define information architecture and user flows
step.action: Create the sitemap — every screen/page with hierarchy:
step.example: Home (Landing Page) ├── Hero section ├── Features section ├── Pricing section ├── Testimonials └── Footer Dashboard ├── Sidebar navigation ├── Overview (default view) ├── Projects list ├── Project detail └── Settings
step.action: For each screen, define content hierarchy:

list.item: Primary content — what the user came here for
Primary action — the ONE thing you want them to do (CTA)

step.action: Map critical user flows step by step:

list.item: Error paths: what fails, how to recover
Empty states: first use, no data

step.action: Define all screen states:

step.critical: Structure before aesthetics — wireframes validate the IA
step.action: For each screen, create ASCII wireframes for:

list.item: Tablet (768px) — only if layout changes significantly

rules.rule: Use clear ASCII notation: [Button Label] — button [___placeholder___] — text input (o) Option — radio [x] Checked — checkbox [v Select ▾] — dropdown ┌─────────┐ — card/container │ content │ └─────────┘
Annotate WHY elements are positioned where they are
Show the primary CTA prominently in each screen
Group related elements with visual proximity

step.goal: Select Pencil style archetype and parameters
step.critical: Map every visual decision to a Pencil option — no abstract choices
step.action: Based on brand personality and user research, select:

decision.name: Pencil Style Archetype
decision.instruction: Pick ONE from the 10 styles listed in pencil-reference above
Justify: why does this style fit the brand personality and user context?
Note anti-references: which styles were rejected and why?

decision.name: Color Palette
decision.instruction: Justify: what emotion does this palette convey? How does it serve the brand?
If user has existing brand colors, note them — the Pencil agent can override palette values via set_variables

decision.name: Typography

list.item: headings — display, H1-H3, navigation labels (maps to $--font-primary)
data — numbers, tables, dashboards (if style supports it)

decision.instruction: Justify each choice: what personality does this font convey?

decision.name: Elevation
decision.instruction: Pick ONE shadow style from: Gentle Lift, Sharp Depth, Soft Cloud, Soft Lift
Justify: sharp for precision, soft for approachability, etc.

decision.name: Decorative Imagery
decision.instruction: Pick the imagery approach from the style's options
Describe the imagery tone and subjects

decision.name: Pencil Guide(s)
decision.instruction: Pick the relevant guide(s) the Pencil agent should load:

list.item: Web App — for SaaS, dashboards, admin panels
Landing Page — for marketing, conversion pages
Design System — ALWAYS include this one for component composition
Table — if the product has data tables

step.critical: If user has specific brand colors or fonts, define custom variable values
step.action: List ALL design token overrides the Pencil agent should apply via set_variables:
step.example: Variables to set: --background: "#FAFAFA" (override palette default — user wants slightly warmer) --primary: "#6366F1" (brand purple — overrides palette accent) --font-primary: "Inter" (already selected above) --font-secondary: "Inter" (same font, different weights) --radius-m: 8 (medium roundness for cards and inputs)
step.action: If user needs dark mode, define themed variables:
step.example: Themed variables: --background: light="#FFFFFF", dark="#0A0A0A" --foreground: light="#171717", dark="#EDEDED" --card: light="#FFFFFF", dark="#1A1A1A"
step.action: Define any custom semantic tokens not in Pencil's defaults:
For each screen in the sitemap, list:

screen-brief.field: Pencil frame dimensions (width x height or fit_content)

Components needed — map to Pencil's Design System guide components: - Sidebar (with nav items) - Card (with slots: header, content, actions) - Table (Table > Row > Cell hierarchy) - Button (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive) - Input (text, search, select) - Modal/Dialog - Toast/Notification - Badge/Tag - Avatar - Tabs - Pagination
Content — actual text content for headings, labels, CTAs, descriptions
Icons — specific icon names from lucide/feather/Material Symbols
Images — describe what images should show (for G() AI generation)
States to design — which states of this screen need separate frames?

step.action: Define interaction patterns (for implementation reference, not Pencil):

list.item: Card hover effect (shadow lift? border highlight?)
Modal entrance/exit animation (fade+scale? slide from bottom?)
Loading patterns (skeleton shimmer? spinner? progress bar?)
Scroll behavior (sticky header? reveal on scroll up?)

step.action: Define duration scale:
step.critical: Apply these animation constraints — non-negotiable for performance:

animation-rules.rule: Only animate transform and opacity — never animate layout properties (width, height, top, left, margin, padding)
Use will-change sparingly — only on elements that will actually animate, remove after animation completes
All durations between 150ms-500ms — below 100ms feels instant (pointless), above 500ms feels sluggish
Easing: ease-out for entrances (decelerating), ease-in for exits (accelerating), ease-in-out for state changes
Stagger delay for lists: 30-50ms between items, max 8 items staggered (beyond that, animate as group)
MANDATORY: all animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion — provide instant transitions as fallback
Target: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) < 0.1 — animations must not cause layout shifts

step.critical: This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Pencil agent
It must contain ZERO ambiguity — every decision is made, every value is specified
step.action: Save to: $PROJECT_ROOT/_flow_output/planning-artifacts/design-spec.md
step.template:
# Design Spec — {{project_name}}
> This spec is the complete brief for the Pencil agent. Follow it step by step.
## 1. Project Context
- **Product type**: [SaaS / Landing Page / Mobile App / ...] - **Core proposition**: [one sentence] - **Target users**: [who, context, goals] - **Business goal**: [primary metric] - **Tech stack**: [for implementation reference]
## 2. Pencil Configuration
### Style Archetype - **Style**: [exact name from Pencil's 10 styles] - **Why**: [justification]
### Style Parameters ```yaml colorPalette: "[exact palette name]" headings: "[font name]" body: "[font name]" captions: "[font name]" data: "[font name]" # if style supports it roundness: "Basic Roundness" elevation: "[elevation name]" # if style supports it decorativeImagery: "[option]" # if style supports it ```
### Pencil Guides to Load 1. `get_guidelines({ category: "guide", name: "[Guide Name]" })` 2. `get_guidelines({ category: "guide", name: "Design System" })`
### Design Token Overrides ```yaml # Call set_variables with these values AFTER loading the style variables: --background: "[hex]" --foreground: "[hex]" --primary: "[hex]" --secondary: "[hex]" --card: "[hex]" --border: "[hex]" --muted-foreground: "[hex]" --destructive: "[hex]" --font-primary: "[font name]" --font-secondary: "[font name]" --radius-m: [number] --radius-pill: [number] # Custom tokens: --color-success: "[hex]" --color-warning: "[hex]" --color-error: "[hex]" ```
### Dark Mode (if applicable) ```yaml themes: axes: mode: ["light", "dark"] variables: --background: { light: "[hex]", dark: "[hex]" } --foreground: { light: "[hex]", dark: "[hex]" } --card: { light: "[hex]", dark: "[hex]" } # ... etc ```
## 3. Sitemap
[Tree structure of all screens/pages]
## 4. User Flows
### Flow: [Name] 1. [Step] → [Screen] 2. [Step] → [Screen] ...
## 5. Screen Specifications
### Screen: [Name] - **Purpose**: [why this screen exists] - **Frame**: [width]x[height or fit_content] - **Layout**: [vertical/horizontal] - **Pencil guide rules**: [specific rules from the guide that apply]
#### Structure ``` [ASCII wireframe with annotations] ```
#### Components | Component | Type | Content | Icon | Notes | |-----------|------|---------|------|-------| | Header | frame, horizontal | — | — | sticky, $--background | | Logo | text | "ProductName" | — | $--font-primary, 20px, bold | | Nav Item | text | "Dashboard" | lucide/layout-dashboard | $--muted-foreground | | CTA | frame + text | "Get Started" | — | $--primary bg, white text | | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
#### States - **Empty**: [what to show, illustration prompt for G()] - **Loading**: [skeleton pattern] - **Error**: [error message, recovery action]
#### Images (for G() operations) | Element | Type | Prompt | |---------|------|--------| | Hero background | "ai" | "[detailed prompt]" | | Avatar | "stock" | "[description]" |
[Repeat for each screen]
## 6. Responsive Strategy
- **Mobile (375px)**: [what changes — stacked layout, hidden sidebar, bottom nav] - **Tablet (768px)**: [what changes] - **Desktop (1440px)**: [full layout as designed]
## 7. Micro-interactions (for implementation)
| Interaction | Animation | Duration | Easing | |-------------|-----------|----------|--------| | Button hover | scale 1.02, shadow lift | 150ms | ease-out | | Card hover | shadow.md → shadow.lg | 200ms | ease-out | | Modal open | fade + scale from 0.95 | 300ms | ease-out | | Toast enter | slide from top | 300ms | ease-out | | Page transition | fade | 200ms | ease-in-out |
## 8. Accessibility & Quality Checklist
- **Target**: WCAG AA - **Focus ring**: 2px solid $--primary, 2px offset - **Tab order**: [key navigation paths] - **Reduced motion**: disable all animations, instant transitions
### Quality Gates (must pass before spec is final) | Criterion | Threshold | How to verify | |-----------|-----------|---------------| | Color contrast (text) | ≥ 4.5:1 normal, ≥ 3:1 large (18px+) | Check $--foreground on $--background, $--muted-foreground on $--card | | Color contrast (UI) | ≥ 3:1 for interactive elements | Check $--primary on $--background, $--border visibility | | Touch targets | ≥ 44x44px on mobile | All buttons, links, inputs, icons | | Line length | 45-75 characters | Body text containers | | Line height | ≥ 1.5 for body text | $--font-secondary usage | | Animation duration | 150-500ms range | All micro-interactions | | CLS budget | < 0.1 | No layout-shifting animations | | Focus visible | Every interactive element | Tab through all screens mentally | | Color not sole indicator | Status uses icon + color | Error, success, warning states |
## 9. Pencil Execution Order
> The Pencil agent should follow this order:
1. Open/create .pen file 2. Load style: `get_guidelines({ category: "style", name: "[style]", params: {...} })` 3. Load guide(s): `get_guidelines({ category: "guide", name: "[guide]" })` 4. Set variable overrides: `set_variables({ variables: {...} })` 5. Design Screen 1: [name] — [priority: high/medium/low] 6. Design Screen 2: [name] — [priority] 7. ... [in order of importance] 8. Verify each screen: `get_screenshot` after completion 9. Audit consistency: `search_all_unique_properties` for colors, fonts, spacing

step.goal: Present summary and get user validation
step.action: Present a concise summary:

list.item: Pencil style chosen + color palette + why

step.action: Ask for validation:

list.item: Does the visual direction feel right?
Are the screens complete — anything missing?

step.action: Iterate until user approves
step.output:
Design spec saved: _flow_output/planning-artifacts/design-spec.md
Next step: open Pencil and run the spec. The Pencil agent should: 1. get_guidelines → load style + guide 2. set_variables → apply tokens 3. batch_design → build each screen 4. get_screenshot → verify each screen

decision-framework.test: Can this be directly executed with Pencil's tools?
Would the Pencil agent need to ask a follow-up question?
Are all screens, states, components, and tokens specified?
Is every visual choice backed by user/brand reasoning?
Do all design tokens pass the accessibility and quality checklist in Section 8?
Do the UX patterns match the product type's domain heuristics?
decision-framework.rule: If a decision fails any test, refine it before including it in the spec.
